title: AI Roadmap for Network Engineers
description: A structured roadmap outlining the journey from networking fundamentals to AI-driven intelligent network operations.
outcome: Created a clear learning path for network engineers transitioning into AI and intelligent networking.
details:
This project defines a structured career path for network engineers transitioning into AI-driven networking.

Stage 1: Networking Foundation — Build strong expertise in TCP/IP, routing, switching, BGP, MPLS, SD-WAN, and network security. Understanding protocols and real-world troubleshooting is critical.

Stage 2: Automation — Learn Python and network automation tools such as APIs (REST/JSON), Netmiko, NAPALM, and Ansible to automate repetitive tasks like configuration, validation, and monitoring.

Stage 3: Data — Work with network telemetry, logs, and time-series data. Focus on parsing CLI outputs, log analysis, and building dashboards for visibility.

Stage 4: AI & Machine Learning — Learn ML basics and apply them to networking use cases such as anomaly detection, traffic prediction, and intelligent alerting.

Stage 5: Intelligent Networks — Move towards self-healing and AI-driven networks with automated decision-making, capacity planning, and predictive operations.

This roadmap bridges traditional networking with modern AI-driven operations and provides a practical learning path for engineers aiming to become network architects or AI-focused network specialists.

title: AI Network Health Checker
description: Parses router outputs such as show bgp summary, show processes cpu, and interface status to detect BGP issues, high CPU, CRC errors, and down interfaces.
outcome: Built a mini NOC-style automation workflow for health reporting.
details: This project reads CLI-style router outputs and checks for BGP Active state, high CPU utilization, CRC errors, and down interfaces. It uses Python basics such as split(), strip(), loops, and conditions to build a practical monitoring script for network engineers.

title: Router Output File Analyzer
description: Reads saved router output files such as bgp.txt, cpu.txt, and interface.txt, then generates a consolidated health summary automatically.
outcome: Converted manual CLI review into repeatable automation.
details: This project moves from hardcoded router output to real text files. It reads multiple files, parses them independently, and combines the results into one health report. This is a strong foundation for later building AI-assisted NOC tools.
